description: Provides food establishment inspection scores performed within the last 3 years. Inspections are conducted in accordance with the Texas Food Establishment Rules (TFER) and City of Austin Codes. Inspections are completed by Environmental Health Officers working for Austin Public Health (APH) Environmental Health Services Division (EHSD).

name: Unclaimed Property
description: Visit the City's Unclaimed Property Site at https://www.austintexas.gov/services/search-unclaimed-property for information about how to submit a claim if you find your name on the list. Every year, various departments of the City of Austin report unclaimed cash and valuables, from uncashed checks, deposits, refunds, overpayments or any other transactions creating a credit balance valued at $100 or less. Contact: 512-974-7890 or 512-974-1384

name: Austin Animal Center Outcomes (10/01/2013 to 05/05/2025)
description: Animal Center Outcomes from Oct, 1st 2013 to May 5th 2025. Outcomes represent the status of animals as they leave the Animal Center. All animals receive a unique Animal ID during intake. Annually over 90% of animals entering the center, are adopted, transferred to rescue or returned to their owners. The Outcomes data set reflects that Austin, TX. is the largest "No Kill" city in the country.

name: Austin Animal Center Intakes (10/01/2013 to 05/05/2025)
description: Animal Center Intakes from Oct, 1st 2013 to May, 5th 2025. Intakes represent the status of animals as they arrive at the Animal Center. All animals receive a unique Animal ID during intake. Annually over 90% of animals entering the center, are adopted, transferred to rescue or returned to their owners.

Video is NOT recorded or retained of daily traffic. Traffic cameras are owned and operated by Austin Transportation & Public Works. You may also be interested in our traffic signal operations dashboards, available at https://data.mobility.austin.gov For information about Austin's Mobility Management Center, visit: http://www.austintexas.gov/department/a

name: Water Quality Sampling Data
description: Data collected to assess water quality conditions in the natural creeks, aquifers and lakes in the Austin area. This is raw data, provided directly from our Water Resources Monitoring database (WRM) and should be considered provisional. Data may or may not have been reviewed by project staff. A map of site locations can be found by searching for LOCATION.WRM_SAMPLE_SITES; you may then use those WRM_SITE_IDs to filter in this dataset using the field SAMPLE_SITE_NO.

NEW indicates the transaction is newly reported,

columns.description: If the record is a "temporary" record, which means that the record was manually created in the database by Vision Zero staff, as opposed to the record having been created from our integration with the TxDOT CRIS database. Because there can be a lag time of weeks, even months, before law enforcement investigators submit their crash report to TxDOT, the Vision Zero team occasionally creates temporary records so that our reporting metrics are more timely/accurate. The expectation is that a temporary record will be deleted from the database once the official crash report has been received.

description: This dataset reports on the operation state of traffic signals in Austin, TX. Traffic signals enter flash mode when something is preventing the signal from operating normally. This is typically the result of a power surge, power outage, or damage to signal equipment. A signal may also be intentionally placed into flash mode for maintenance purposes or be scheduled to flash overnight. You can view an interactive map of flashing traffic signals here: https://data.mobility.austin.gov/signal-monitor

description: Extra bags of trash that do not fit in your trash cart with the lid closed must be placed next to the trash cart and tagged with an Extra Trash Sticker, which can be purchased at H-E-B, Randall's and most local grocery stores for $4 + tax. Extra bags without a sticker will be charged a per-bag fee of $9.60 + tax. If you find that you often set out extra bagged garbage, consider switching to a larger cart for $15 (plus applicable monthly cart fee).

columns.description: Coded value for victim gender, can be male, female, gender diverse or unknown.

columns.name: Case Report Number
columns.description: The case report number for the incident may have many crimes associated with it, as distinguished by Offense ID. The case report number typically is a four-digit year, three-digit day of year, and system generated four-digit occurrence number. Case report numbers that do not follow this pattern may indicate a report intake method outside of computer aided dispatch.

description: This table contains data on maintenance of the department response fleet – ambulances, command trucks, and utility vehicles. It includes the number of vehicles due for preventative maintenance each month, and the percentage that are serviced. The data is broken out by the three vehicle types in addition to describing overall performance. When no units are due for service in a month, the "Percent Serviced" column will show a null (blank) value for that month.

name: APD Computer Aided Dispatch Incidents

columns.name: Final Problem Description
columns.description: A description of the ultimate issue that was meant to be addressed by the CAD incident. This may differ from the initial problem description based on additional information that comes to light during the call-taking and response processes. Certain final problem descriptions may describe specific criminal offenses from the Texas Penal Code.

columns.name: Response Datetime
columns.description: The date and time that the 911 call-takers Emergency Call Taker (ECT) screen opened following the 911 call being answered in the case of dispatched incidents. In the case of officer-initiated incidents, this field indicates the time the incident was created and sent to the incident queue if it was created via the officer's onboard computer or the time the dispatcher opened their ECT screen and created the incident if the officer created the incident through dispatch.

columns.name: Response Day of Week
columns.description: The three-character day of week of the Response Datetime.

columns.name: Mental Health Flag
columns.description: A flag indicating whether a given incident was mental-health related or not. A given incident may be flagged as mental-health-related due to the initial problem description, the final problem description, the call disposition, because a crisis counselor was assigned to the call, because the caller asked for mental health services, because a 911 call taker assessed mental health played a role in the call or because the subject of a use of force related to the incident displayed "Emotionally Disturbed Person" conduct. Please note that multiple of the preceding conditions are often true for mental-health related calls. Problem descriptions related to mental health may indicate the call is related to an "Emotionally Disturbed Person" or an attempted suicide. Call dispositions that indicate a call is mental-health-related will contain an "MH" in the call disposition description and indicate that a responding officer assessed that mental health had a role in the incident.

columns.name: Incident Number
columns.description: An unique integer identifying a computer-aided dispatch incident, consisting of a two-digit year number, three-digit day-of-year and four-digit incident-count number.

columns.name: Final Problem Category
columns.description: A general category describing the problem that the CAD incident was meant to address based on the Final Problem Description value.

columns.name: Census Block Group
columns.description: Shortened Block Group Name based on the Census Bureau's Geo ID. State digits have been removed due to system limitations.

columns.name: Geo ID
columns.description: A Census Block Group identifier; a concatenation of the current state FIPS code, county FIPS code, census tract code, and block group number.

columns.name: Unit Time on Scene
columns.description: The total amount of time spent on scene by all APD units in seconds.

columns.name: Number of Units Arrived
columns.description: A count of the number of APD units that arrived on scene in response to the CAD incident. Please note that some units may consist of more than one APD officer, while certain units may also include civilian personnel such as crime scene technicians. Moreover, this count may include a variety of type of personnel including patrol officers, investigators, supervisors and public information officers if such personnel are required for the incident response.

columns.name: Initial Problem Category
columns.description: A general category describing the problem that the CAD incident was meant to address based on the Initial Problem Description value.

columns.name: First Unit Arrived Datetime
columns.description: The date and time that the first APD unit arrived on scene to the incident. This is the endpoint for response time calculations. Multiple units may arrive on a single incident, while individual units may be made up of one or more officers.

columns.name: Response Month
columns.description: Month incident occurred.

columns.name: Other Injured/Killed Count
columns.description: The number of persons seriously injured or killed in an incident related to this CAD incident where there was also a use of force against an individual with mental illness where either the subject of that use of force or the officer involved in that use of force were seriously injured or killed.

columns.name: Incident Type
columns.description: A flag indicating whether a given incident was dispatched or officer-initiated. Dispatched incidents are received via 911 calls while officer-initiated incidents are created by officers in the field.

columns.name: Subject Injured/Killed Count
columns.description: A count of the subjects of a use of force who were seriously injured or killed in a use of force related to this CAD incident where those subjects had a mental illness.

columns.name: Response Year
columns.description: Year incident occurred.

columns.name: Initial Problem Description
columns.description: A preliminary description of the problem meant to be addressed by the CAD incident based on either the information given to the 911 call taker in the case of dispatched incidents, or information collected by officers in the case of officer-initiated incidents. Initial problem descriptions may differ from final problem descriptions based on additional information that comes to light during the call-taking and response processes.

columns.name: Call Closed Datetime
columns.description: The date and time that the incident was closed, indicating that all units have left the scene and it is no longer active.

columns.name: Officer Injured/Killed Count
columns.description: A count of officers seriously injured or killed in a use of force related to this CAD incident where the subject of the use of force was a person with mental illness.

columns.name: Report Written Flag
columns.description: A flag indicating that a responding unit wrote a report in the Records Management System based on this CAD incident. Note that a report being written does not necessarily mean that a criminal offense took place.

columns.name: Priority Level
columns.description: The priority level assigned to the incident at the time of the first officer's arrival. Priority levels range between 0 and 4, with 0 being the highest priority and 4 being the lowest priority.

columns.name: Call Disposition Description
columns.description: A description of the outcome of the call.

columns.name: Sector
columns.description: The Austin Police Department patrol sector where the incident occurred.

columns.name: Response Hour
columns.description: An integer representing the hour of day of the Response Datetime

columns.name: Response Time
columns.description: The amount of time between when the 911 call was answered and when the first officer arrived on scene in seconds. Please note that this field is not applicable to officer-initiated incidents and is left blank for such incidents.

columns.name: Council District
columns.description: The council district where the offense occurred.

description: DATASET DESCRIPTION This dataset contains information on both 911 calls (usually referred to as Calls for Service or Dispatched Incidents) and officer-initiated incidents recorded in the Computer Aided Dispatch (CAD) system. These are differentiated by the Incident Type field, defined below. This data excludes records that were cancelled after being identified as duplicates of the same incident, such as when two 911 calls are made for the same incident. It also excludes records that were cancell

name: Land Use Inventory Detailed
description: The land inventory is based on several sources. The polygon geography is taken from appraisal district parcel layers merged together. A land use inventory is performed by classifying land according to a coding system that reflects the primary improvements (buildings or structures) on each parcel. Most of the land use information is attached through a GIS Union from past land use inventories. Undeveloped parcels are checked against building permit, aerial photos, and appraisal records, generally

name: EMS - Communications Measures

columns.name: 911 Call Count
columns.description: Count of phone calls received through 911 system.

columns.name: MPDS Triage Compliance Target
columns.description: Performance target for compliance with Medical Priority Dispatch System (MPDS) standards.

columns.name: Grade of Service Target
columns.description: Performance target for percent of 911 calls answered within 10 seconds of initial ring.

columns.name: Call Processing Interval
columns.description: Average amount of time required to dispatch the first unit to an incident, measured from the time that the 911 call is answered.  This value is reported in seconds.

columns.name: Month Key
columns.description: Year and month for record in <yyyymm> format (e.g. 201407).  This format is useful for sorting records.

columns.name: Grade of Service
columns.description: Percent of 911 calls answered within 10 seconds of initial ring.

columns.name: Calls Processed Count
columns.description: Count of calls included in Call Processing Interval indicators.

columns.name: Call Processing Interval Target
columns.description: Performance target for Call Processing Interval.  This value is reported in seconds.

columns.name: Month-Year
columns.description: Month-year for report.  This column contains the first day of the month.  It is formatted to present only month and year.

columns.name: Call Processing Compliance Target
columns.description: Performance target for Call Processing Compliance.  This value is reported in percent of calls.

columns.name: MPDS Triage Compliance
columns.description: Communications Center compliance with Medical Priority Dispatch System (MPDS) standards.

columns.name: Call Processing Compliance
columns.description: Percent of calls processed that are completed in 90 seconds or less.

description: Data contained in this table documents Austin-Travis County EMS (ATCEMS) Communications Center workload and performance. Data contained in this table comes from several sources: • 911 Call Count and Grade of Service are obtained from the ECaTS reporting system provided by the Capital Area Council of Governments (CAPCOG). • Call Processing Interval is calculated using data from the department Computer-Aided Dispatch (CAD) data warehouse. • MPDS Compliance is calculated by the Advanced Quality Ass

name: ELEVATION_contours_2021
description: ELEVATION.contours_2021 Summary The Texas Natural Resources Information System (TNRIS) contracted Sanborn to fly LiDAR in March of 2021. TNRIS then created the contours in the Spring of 2022 using Global Mapper. Description This layer represents contour elevation lines as of the March 2021. The contours are derived from LiDAR data, collected in the March 2021. Contours were generated using Global Mapper, sample spacing used to create the contours is consistent with the Nominal Point Spacing (NPS

name: APD Arrests
provider: City of Austin, Texas - Austin Police - data.austintexas.gov

columns.name: Arrest Occurred Date
columns.description: The date on which the arrest took place. The arrest report is written on the day the arrest occurred, which may differ from the date the arrest incident occurred.

columns.name: Arrest Sector
columns.description: The Austin Police Department patrol sector where the arrest occurred.

columns.name: Arrest Type Description
columns.description: Describes the type of arrest, including on view arrest, summoned/cited, or taken into custody.

columns.name: Warrant Flag (TRUE/FALSE)
columns.description: An indicator used to indicate whether a subject was arrested and taken into custody. A value of 'TRUE' means the subject was arrested and taken into custody. A value of 'FALSE' means the subject was not arrested and taken into custody.

columns.name: Cite/Release Flag (1/0)
columns.description: An indicator used to identify whether a subject was summoned or given a citation. A value of '1' means that the subject was either summoned or given a citation. A value of '0' means that the subject was not summoned or given a citation.

columns.name: Subject Race/Ethnicity
columns.description: A person's self-identification with one or more social groups.

columns.name: Census Block Group
columns.description: The Census Block Group where the incident occurred.

columns.name: Arrest Report Number
columns.description: The arrest report number is unique for an arrest. The arrest of one person may clear several cases and crimes, and the arrest of many persons may clear only one case.

columns.name: Warrant Flag (1/0)
columns.description: An indicator used to indicate whether a subject was arrested and taken into custody. A value of '1' means the subject was arrested and taken into custody. A value of '0' means the subject was not arrested and taken into custody.

columns.name: Arrest Charges
columns.description: The charge for which a subject was arrested.

columns.name: Subject Gender
columns.description: Gender identity refers to a person's sense of their own gender, whether male, female, or another category such as non-binary.

columns.name: Cite/Release Flag (TRUE/FALSE)
columns.description: An indicator used to identify whether a subject was summoned or given a citation. A value of 'TRUE' means that the subject was either summoned or given a citation. A value of 'FALSE' means that the subject was not summoned or given a citation.

description: DATASET DESCRIPTION: When an officer finds it necessary to arrest an individual, such as upon witnessing a crime, having probable cause, or acting on a judge-issued arrest warrant, they are required to write an arrest report. The arrest report details the conditions of the arrest and directly pertains to the individual in question. Additionally, it includes specific details of the charges associated with the arrest. description: The U.S. Census defines Asian Americans as individuals having origins in any of the original peoples of the Far East, Southeast Asia, or the Indian subcontinent (U.S. Office of Management and Budget, 1997). As a broad racial category, Asian Americans are the fastest-growing minority group in the United States (U.S. Census Bureau, 2012). The growth rate of 42.9% in Asian Americans between 2000 and 2010 is phenomenal given that the corresponding figure for the U.S. total population is only 9.3% (s

name: EMS - Quarterly OMD Clinical Performance Indicators

columns.name: Count - BGL Test for Seizure Patients
columns.description: Count of seizure patients who receive a blood glucose level assessment.

columns.name: Count – ACS Patients
columns.description: Count of Acute Coronary Syndrome (ACS) patients.  This includes patients with a Primary Impression of “Acute Coronary Syndrome” or “STEMI,” and patients for whom a STEMI Alert is called.  This group will be larger than the group for CPI 3 – Scene interval for STEMI Alerts.

columns.name: Count – ACS Patient ASA Administration
columns.description: Count of ACS patients who receive aspirin (ASA).

columns.name: Percent – ACS Patient ASA Administration
columns.description: Percent of ACS patients who receive aspirin.

columns.name: ACS Patient ASA Administration Target
columns.description: Target performance level for aspirin administration to ACS patients.

columns.name: Count - AMS Patients
columns.description: Count of patients with a primary impression suggestive of Altered Mental Status (AMS).

columns.name: Count - BGL Test for AMS Patients
columns.description: Count of AMS patients who receive a blood glucose level (BGL) assessment.

columns.name: Percent - BGL Test for AMS Patients
columns.description: Percent of AMS patients who receive a blood glucose level assessment.

columns.name: AMS Patient BGL Test Target
columns.description: Target performance level for blood glucose assessment of AMS patients.

columns.name: Percent - BGL Test for Seizure Patients
columns.description: Percent of seizure patients who receive a blood glucose level assessment.

columns.name: Seizure Patient BGL Test Target
columns.description: Target performance level for blood glucose assessment of seizure patients.

columns.name: Count - Seizure Patients
columns.description: Count of patients with a primary impression of “Seizure – Other Convulsions,” “Seizure Febrile,” or “Seizure Postictal State.”

columns.name: Count - Stroke Patients
columns.description: Count of stroke patients.  This definition includes all patients with Primary Impression of stroke, regardless of whether the patient qualified as a Stroke Alert, as well as those for whom a Stroke Alert is called.  This group will be larger than the group for CPI 2—Scene interval for Stroke Alerts.

columns.name: Count - BGL Test for Stroke Patients
columns.description: Count of stroke patients who receive a blood glucose level assessment.

columns.name: Percent - BGL Test for Stroke Patients
columns.description: Percent of stroke patients who receive a blood glucose level assessment.

columns.name: Count - Trauma Alert Scene Interval Compliance
columns.description: Count of Trauma Alert patients with a scene interval less than 15 minutes.  Scene interval starts when the first ATCEMS unit arrives on scene, and ends when ATCEMS personnel depart the scene.

columns.name: Percent - STEMI Alert Scene Interval Compliance
columns.description: Percent of STEMI Alert patients with a scene interval less than 15 minutes.

columns.name: 90th Percentile Stroke Alert Scene Interval
columns.description: 90th percentile scene interval for included Stroke Alert patients, measured in decimal minutes.  Scene interval starts when the first ATCEMS unit arrives on scene, and ends when ATCEMS personnel depart the scene.

columns.name: Stroke Patient BGL Test Target
columns.description: Target performance level for blood glucose assessment of stroke patients.

columns.name: Fiscal Quarter Key
columns.description: Row identifier – numeric representation of fiscal quarter in <yyyyqq> format.

columns.name: Fiscal Quarter
columns.description: Year and fiscal quarter for record in <yyyy-qq> format (e.g. 2014-Q1).

columns.name: Fiscal Quarter Start Date
columns.description: First day of fiscal quarter, presented in <mmm yyyy> format.

columns.name: Fiscal Quarter End Date
columns.description: Last day of fiscal quarter, presented in <mmm yyyy> format.

columns.name: Count - Trauma Alerts
columns.description: Count of Trauma Alert patients.  In this setting, “Trauma Alert” refers to injured patients who meet Physiological or Anatomical criteria for transport to a Trauma Center per City of Austin/Travis County EMS System Clinical Operating Guidelines.

columns.name: Percent - Trauma Alert Scene Interval Compliance
columns.description: Percent of Trauma Alert patients with a scene interval less than 15 minutes.

columns.name: Trauma Alert Scene Interval Compliance Target
columns.description: Target performance level for compliance with scene interval goal for Trauma Alert patients.

columns.name: 90th Percentile Trauma Scene Time
columns.description: 90th percentile scene interval for included Trauma Alert patients, measured in decimal minutes.  Scene interval starts when the first ATCEMS unit arrives on scene, and ends when ATCEMS personnel depart the scene.

columns.name: 90th Percentile Trauma Alert Scene Interval Target
columns.description: Target performance level for 90th percentile scene interval for Trauma Alert patients, measured in decimal minutes.

columns.name: Count - Stroke Alerts
columns.description: Count of Stroke Alert patients.  Not all stroke patients meet criteria for calling a Stroke Alert, so this group will be smaller than the group for CPI 7 – Blood Glucose Test for Stroke Patients.

columns.name: Count - Stroke Alert Scene Interval Compliance
columns.description: Count of Stroke Alert patients with a scene interval less than 15 minutes.  Scene interval starts when the first ATCEMS unit arrives on scene, and ends when ATCEMS personnel depart the scene.

columns.name: Percent - Stroke Alert Scene Interval Compliance
columns.description: Percent of Stroke Alert patients with a scene interval less than 15 minutes.

columns.name: Stroke Alert Scene Interval Compliance Target
columns.description: Target performance level for compliance with scene interval goal for Stroke Alert patients.

columns.name: 90th Percentile Stroke Alert Scene Interval Target
columns.description: Target performance level for 90th percentile scene interval for Stroke Alert patients, measured in decimal minutes.

columns.name: Count - STEMI Alerts
columns.description: Count of STEMI Alert patients.  STEMI Alert patients are a subset of ACS patients; therefore, this count will be smaller than the count for CPI 4 – Aspirin Administration to ACS Patients.

columns.name: Count - STEMI Alert Scene Interval Compliance
columns.description: Count of STEMI Alert patients with a scene interval less than 15 minutes.  Scene interval starts when the first ATCEMS unit arrives on scene, and ends when ATCEMS personnel depart the scene.

columns.name: STEMI Alert Scene Interval Compliance Target
columns.description: Target performance level for compliance with scene interval goal for STEMI Alert patients.

columns.name: 90th Percentile STEMI Alert Scene Interval
columns.description: 90th percentile scene interval for included STEMI Alert patients, measured in decimal minutes.  Scene interval starts when the first ATCEMS unit arrives on scene, and ends when ATCEMS personnel depart the scene.

columns.name: 90th Percentile STEMI Alert Scene Interval Target
columns.description: Target performance level for 90th percentile scene interval for STEMI Alert patients, measured in decimal minutes.

name: Bluetooth Travel Sensors - Individual Traffic Match Files (ITMF)

columns.name: End Time

columns.name: Filter Identifier
columns.description: The numeric code of the filtering algorithm used in the outlier filter for the roadway segment. See the host documentation section titled “Algorithm Configuration” for more information.

columns.name: Speed (Miles Per Hour)
columns.description: The speed in miles per hour between the origin and the destination sensors.

columns.name: Device Address
columns.description: The unique address of the device that was read by the field software. For security, the source MAC address is discarded and replaced with a unique address that is reset daily.

columns.name: Match Validity
columns.description: Indicates whether the sensor server classified the traffic data sample as being valid or invalid based on the filtering algorithm and minimum/maximum allowable speeds applied to the roadway segment. Values are valid or invalid.

columns.name: Travel Time Seconds
columns.description: The travel time in seconds from the origin to the destination sensor.

columns.name: Record ID
columns.description: The unique record identifer generated as an MD5 hash of the row contents.

columns.name: Day of Week
columns.description: The name of the day of the week at the time the device address was recorded at the origin sensor.

columns.name: Start Time
columns.description: The time the device address was recorded at the origin sensor.

columns.name: Origin Reader Identifier
columns.description: The unique identifier assigned to origin sensor that recorded a device address match.

name: Watershed Reach Index and Problem Scores

columns.name: Year of Observation
columns.description: The City of Austin Fiscal year the data points are associated with. FY 2014 started on 01-OCT-2013 and ended 30-SEP-2014.

columns.name: Water Quality Problem
columns.description: Water Quality problem score (creeks only). 100 = worst condition. Water quality worst case.

columns.name: Animal Waste Problem
columns.description: Animal Problem score (creeks only). 100 = worst condition. Pet waste.

columns.name: Vegetation
columns.description: Vegetation Index score (for lakes only). 100 = best condition.

columns.name: Stability Problem
columns.description: Stability problem score (creeks only). 100 = worst condition. Stream bank failures.

columns.name: Sediment
columns.description: Sediment Index score. 100 = best condition. Metals, pcbs, pesticides average.

columns.name: Non-Contact Recreation
columns.description: Non Contact Recreation Index score (creeks only). 100 = best condition. Aesthetics, odor, safety.

columns.name: Eutrophication
columns.description: Eutrophication Index score (for lakes only). 100 = best condition. In general, lower chlorophyll-a abundance and lower proportion of blue-green algae lead to a higher score to represent a superior trophic condition.

columns.name: Overall
columns.description: Overall Index Score. 100 = best condition.  Average of other index scores. Problem scores not included.

columns.name: Index Source Type
columns.description: EII - Environmental Integrity Index or ALI - Austin Lakes Index

columns.name: Riparian Vegetation Problem
columns.description: Riparian Vegetation problem score (creeks only). 100 = worst condition. Not enough riparian cover.

columns.name: Litter Problem
columns.description: Litter problem score (creeks only). 100 = worst condition. Trash

columns.name: Fertilizer Problem
columns.description: Fertilizer problem score (creeks only). 100 = worst condition. Nitrate.

columns.name: Construction Runoff Problem
columns.description: Construction TSS Problem score (creeks only). 100 = worst condition.  Erosion and Sedimentation controls failure.

columns.name: Contact Recreation
columns.description: Contact Recreation Index score (for creeks only). 100 = best condition.  (bacteria)

columns.name: Index Phase
columns.description: For streams only - one of two phases.  Lakes all collected in same phase.

columns.name: Water Quality
columns.description: Water Quality Index score. 100 = best condition. (nutrients, temp, tss)

columns.name: Watershed Reach
columns.description: Primary Varchar key for this dataset. 1 is most downstream reach and ascends for subsequent upstream reaches in the same watershed.

columns.name: Integrity Score ID
columns.description: Primary Integer Key for this dataset.

columns.name: Watershed ID
columns.description: This is a foreign key to the system-assigned primary key to the  "Official Watershed List"  https://data.austintexas.gov/Government/Watersheds/ec78-i9z5  for the City of Austin.  1 to 1 relation with Watershed_Name. Even if Barton Creek is renamed, it will always have an ID of 1.

columns.name: Sewage Problem
columns.description: Sewage problem score (creeks only). 100 = worst condition. Water Quality problem caused by sewage.

columns.name: Sediment Problem
columns.description: Sediment problem score (creeks only). 100 = worst condition. Worst of the problems set score.

columns.name: Habitat
columns.description: Habitat Index score. 100 = best condition. Instream cover and substrate niches.

columns.name: Aquatic Life
columns.description: Aquatic Life Index score.  100 = best condition. (Bugs, diatoms abundance, diversity, pollution tolerance and other metrics)

columns.description: The unique identifier of this person record

columns.description: Vehicle mode of person

columns.description: The severity of the involved person's injury, with 1 indicating an incapacitating or suspected serious injury and 4 indicating a fatal injury.

columns.description: The unique identifier of the crash record in the TxDOT CRIS database. This is distinct from our internal ID column, which is used as the primary key for all crash records within our data system.

columns.description: The biological sex of the crash victim, as reported by the investigating law enforcement agency

columns.name: prsn_ethnicity_id
columns.description: Person Ethnicity ID. Coded as: 0: UNKNOWN, 1: WHITE, 2: HISPANIC, 3: BLACK, 4: ASIAN, 5: OTHER, 6: AMER. INDIAN/ALASKAN NATIVE, 94: REPORTED INVALID, 95: NOT REPORTED

columns.name: Is primary person
columns.description: This field indicates if this person was was the driver/operator of a vehicle/mode of transportation involved in the crash. The field is not applicable if the person was a pedestrian.

columns.name: Person Sex ID
columns.description: The biological sex ID of the crash victim, as reported by the investigating law enforcement agency.  where 0 = UNKNOWN; 1 = MALE; 2 = FEMALE, 95 = AUTONOMOUS VEHICLE

columns.name: prsn_age
columns.description: The age of the person involved in the crash.

columns.name: Unit ID
columns.description: The unique ID identifier of the unit record associated with this person record in the Vision Zero databse

columns.name: Is deleted
columns.description: If the record has been "deleted" and is not to included in reporting metrics. The Vision Zero database has a mechanism for soft-deleting records using this boolean flag. Although all records in this dataset are not deleted, a future iteration may included deleted records in order to simplify our data publication process.

columns.name: Crash timestamp
columns.description: The timestamp of this crash, in UTC time

columns.name: Crash Time Stamp (US/Central)
columns.description: The timestamp of the crash, in US/Central time

columns.name: Is temporary record
columns.description: If the record is a "temporary" record, which means that the record was manually created in the database by Vision Zero staff, as opposed to the record having been created from our integration with the TxDOT CRIS database. Because there can be a lag time of weeks, even months, before law enforcement investigators submit their crash report to TxDOT, the Vision Zero team occasionally creates temporary records so that our reporting metrics are more timely/accurate. The expectation is that a temporary record will be deleted from the database once the official crash report has been received.

description: This dataset contains crash victim records for crashes which have occurred within current City of Austin Full Purpose boundaries, inclusive of all public safety jurisdictions, in the last ten years. description: The Council Items Update Report (CIUR) system is a project management tool utilized by City departments to monitor progress of Council-adopted policy initiatives and directives, and track these items to their completion. These items are included under the “Items From Council” agenda heading during each City Council meeting, and typically take the form of a resolution. This public portal allows users to view past Council actions (since 2015) and their respective status updates.

description: View the City of Austin Open Data Terms of Use This is a custom query against the larger data set "Issued Construction Permits". This subset of data is looking only at residential demolition permits issued, including both total demolitions (identified by the "work class" of Demolition) and partial demolitions (identified by the *partial demolition* tag in the folder description). columns.name: cris_crash_id
columns.description: The unique identifier of the crash record in the TxDOT CRIS database. This is distinct from our internal ID column, which is used as the primary key for all crash records within our data system.

columns.name: crash_timestamp_ct
columns.description: The date and time the crash occurred in US/Central time

columns.name: ID
columns.description: The unique ID of this record in the Vision Zero database

columns.name: Location group
columns.description: The location group (1 or 2) is an identifier that roughly classifies crashes as having having occurred on a freeway (group 2) or not (group 1).

columns.name: collsn_desc
columns.description: Collision type

columns.name: onsys_fl
columns.description: Flag indicates whether primary road of crash was on the TxDOT highway system.

columns.name: Crash timestamp
columns.description: The date and time the crash occurred in UTC time

columns.name: john2
columns.description: john2

columns.name: private_dr_fl
columns.description: Flag indicating whether crash occurred on a private drive or road/private property/parking lot.

columns.name: Is deleted
columns.description:
If the record has been "deleted" and is not to included in reporting metrics. The Vision Zero database has a mechanism for soft-deleting records using this boolean flag. 

Although all records in this dataset are not deleted, a future iteration may included deleted records in order to simplify our data publication process

columns.name: Address
columns.description: The address of the crash location

columns.name: Is Temporary Record
columns.description:
If the record is a "temporary" record, which means that the record was manually created in the database by Vision Zero, as opposed to receiving the record from the TxDOT CRIS database.

Because there can be a lag time of weeks, even months, before law enforcement investigators submit their crash report to TxDOT, the Vision Zero team occasionally creates temporary records so that our reporting metrics are more timely/accurate. 

The expectation is that a temporary record will be deleted from the database once the official crash report has been received.

name: Campaign Finance - Credits

columns.name: Political_Contribution_Return
columns.description: If the column is checked X, the incoming credit/gain was originally made by the filer in the form of a political contribution.

columns.name: Date_Reported
columns.description: Date the structured data file was received by the Office of the City Clerk.

columns.name: View_Report
columns.description: A link to the PDF report on which the filer reported the credit/gain/refund/returned contribution, or interested.

columns.name: Out_of_State_PAC
columns.description: If the column is checked X, the incoming credit/gain was received by an out of state PAC.

columns.name: Correction
columns.description: If the column has New or Modify, the credit/gain/refund/returned contribution, or interested was filed as a correction to another report.

columns.name: Recipient
columns.description: Name of the candidate or office holder that is filing the report. This name is a standardized format used by the Office of the City Clerk to ensure all of a filer's reports are accessible under a single name.

columns.name: Credit_Date
columns.description: The date on which the credit/gain/refund was received or the interest was earned.

columns.name: Credit_Amount
columns.description: The exact dollar amount of the credit/gain/refund/returned contribution, or interest.

columns.name: Payer_Type
columns.description: Identifies the payer as either an entity (such as a company, political committee, organization, or other group) or an individual person.

columns.name: Payer_Address
columns.description: Address of individual or entity from whom the credit/gain/refund/returned contribution or interest was received.

columns.name: Credit_Year
columns.description: The date on which the credit/gain/refund was received or the interest was earned.

columns.name: Transaction_Type
columns.description: The type of transaction that occurred.  This column will always be populated by ‘Interest, Credits, Gains, Refunds, and Contributions Returned to Filer.’

columns.name: TRANSACTION_ID
columns.description: Unique ID based upon the concatenation of the Report_ID and Transaction_ID field - acts as a unique key for each individual transaction.

columns.name: In_Kind_Description
columns.description: A description of the credit/gain/refund/returned contribution, or interested.

columns.name: Payer_City_State_Zip
columns.description: City, state, and zip code of individual or entity from whom the credit/gain/refund/returned contribution or interest was received.

columns.name: Report_Filed
columns.description: The type of report on which the filer reported the credit/gain/refund/returned contribution, or interested.

columns.name: Payer
columns.description: The full name of the person of business from whom the credit/gain/refund/returned contribution or interest was received.

description: This table lists any credit, interest, rebate, refund, reimbursement, or return of a deposit fee resulting from the use of a political contribution or an asset purchased with a political contribution reported on the Texas Ethics Commission C/OH Form, Schedule K. For more information about credits, please visit Texas Ethic Commission's Forms & Instructions.

columns.description: The case report number for the incident may have many crimes associated with it, as distinguished by Offense ID. description: The Austin Parks and Recreation System's ranking on the Trust for Public Land ParkScore Index. This index ranks the park systems of the 100 largest cities in the U.S. based on park acreage, park size, park funding, park access, and a variety of other factors. View more details and insights related to this data set on the story page *If a cell is blank, that means APR did not have a response for that year or TPL removed the question for that year's survey.

name: Bar Ditch
description: Bar Ditch - A bar ditch is a man-made, open drainage-way into which excess surface water or groundwater drained from land, storm water runoff, or floodwaters flow either continuously or intermittently. It differs from a ditch in that it is typically less than 18 inches deep and contains driveway culverts of insignificant size (6" - 12"). A single line feature represents the entire line of ditch/culvert representing the bar ditch.This data has been collected as part of a larger project by the Cit

description: This map shows the Equitable Transit-Oriented Development (ETOD) Typologies as attached to the Imagine Austin Comprehensive Plan by City Council on May 30, 2024 through Ordinance No. 20240530-139 (https://services.austintexas.gov/edims/document.cfm?id=430294). name: Top 50 Pages By Pageviews on Austintexas.gov  -
description: This data, exported from Google Analytics displays the most popular 50 pages on Austintexas.gov based on the following: Views: The total number of times the page was viewed. Repeated views of a single page are counted. Bounce Rate: The percentage of single-page visits (i.e. visits in which the person left your site from the entrance page without interacting with the page). *Note: On July 1, 2023, standard Universal Analytics properties will stop processing data.

name: BOUNDARIES_wildland_urban_interface_code
description: Parcels affected by the adoption of the 2015 International Wildland Urban-Interface Code (WUIC), which was adopted by Austin City Council April9, 2020, and implementation beginning January 1st, 2021. Parcels that are within 1.5 miles of a wildland area greater than 750 acres and parcels within 150 feet of a wildland area greater than 40 acres are wildland_urban_interface_code parcels. Parcels designated as "preserves" have been removed and are not subject to the WUI code.Dataset was created in 2

name: Test Austin Crash Demographic Statistics - incomplete and not intended for public analysis

columns.name: unit_id
columns.description: The unique identifier of the unit this person occupied

columns.name: crash_timestamp
columns.description: The timestamp of the crash, in UTC time.

columns.name: CRIS Crash ID
columns.description: The unique identifier of the crash record in the TxDOT CRIS database. This is distinct from our internal ID column, which is used as the primary key for all crash records within our data system.

columns.name: is_temp_record
columns.description: If the record is a "temporary" record, which means that the record was manually created in the database by Vision Zero staff, as opposed to the record having been created from our integration with the TxDOT CRIS database. Because there can be a lag time of weeks, even months, before law enforcement investigators submit their crash report to TxDOT, the Vision Zero team occasionally creates temporary records so that our reporting metrics are more timely/accurate. The expectation is that a temporary record will be deleted from the database once the official crash report has been received.

columns.name: Crash Primary Key
columns.description: The unique ID number of the crash record that this person record is associated with in the Vision Zero database

columns.name: ID
columns.description: The unique ID of this record in the Vision Zero databsae

columns.name: is_primary_person
columns.description: This field indicates if this person was was the driver/operator of a vehicle/mode of transportation involved in the crash. The field is not applicable if the person was a pedestrian.

columns.name: Is Deleted
columns.description: If the record has been "deleted" and is not to included in reporting metrics. The Vision Zero database has a mechanism for soft-deleting records using this boolean flag. Although all records in this dataset are not deleted, a future iteration may included deleted records in order to simplify our data publication process.

description: The Bike Facilities data will serve as the authoritative data for any bicycle infrastructure-related reference and analysis. The Bike Facilities data includes all existing facilities, their level of comfort, as well as recommended facilities and whether they belong to the All Ages and Abilities Network. The data is created from an export of the Comprehensive Transportation Network, and it can join back to it through the Bike Facility ID field.

description: This table contains data describing ATCEMS management of Trauma Alert patients. In this setting, “Trauma Alert” refers to injured patients who meet Physiological or Anatomical criteria for transport to a Trauma Center per City of Austin/Travis County EMS System Clinical Operating Guidelines.

name: Grassland
description: These grasslands were digitized from construction plans, environmental assessments, and City of Austin staff field observations into a versioned SDE database using ArcMap. Actual areas for any particular feature may be fluctuate due to antecedent rainfall, subsequent development activity or the invasion of woody species, Grasslands are not, at the time of this writing, a protected Critical Environmental Feature.

description: Dataset description

name: TRANSPORTATION.pw_current_yr_service_plan
description: This is the street preventative maintenance surface treatments for the current fiscal year. Treatments typically include: overlay, seal coat, slurry seal, fog seal, crack sealing, and microsurfacing.

name: 2020 Census Redistricting Data - Variable Names and Codes
description: These are the variable codes for the datasets released as part of the 2020 decennial census redistricting data.

columns.description: Fiscal Year

description: The data is comprised of the number of domestic and international non-stop destinations from the Austin airport by fiscal year. A nonstop destination is when you fly straight from one airport to another, with no stops along the way. AUS receives an annual report from the Cirium DIIO database. AUS monitors the non-stop destinations because of its implications for passenger growth. View more details and insights related to this data set on the story page

name: Austin Transportation Department Visitor Log For Ordinance No. 20160922-005

columns.name: Auto Signout
columns.description: All visitors who have not signed out by 11:45pm are signed out automatically. This field indicates if the visitor was signed out automatically.

description: City Code §4-8-8(C) now requires departments to obtain written disclosure of specific information when engaging in face-to-face, scheduled meetings with visitors who meet specific criteria. This is the visitor log for visitors who meet that criteria at Austin Transportation and Public Works office locations at: - 3701 Lake Austin Blvd ("LCRA") - 1501 Toomey Rd. ("Toomey Rd") - 1111 Rio Grande St. ("Rio Grande") Related Ordinance: http://www.austintexas.gov/edims/document.cfm?id=265293

name: Capital Open Budget
description: The capital budget is used for one-time infrastructure and improvement projects. The following data provide appropriation-to-date and expenses-to-date metrics for active capital projects, which can be funded through multiple fiscal year capital budgets. These metrics can be drilled down to the department, project, and subproject levels.

name: Revenue Open Budget - revised
description: The revenue budget provides the funds to support the City's operating budget. Please visit https://data.austintexas.gov/stories/s/Key-Terms-and-Facts-about-Open-Budget-ATX/capb-cbrm/ for more information.

name: Grow Zones
description: Areas that are within City of Austin publicly-owned land that have been identfied as Grow Zones in a collaboration between Parks and Recreation and Watershed Protection Departments. Contains primary contact information, acreage, watershed designations and a description of the prescribed maintenance regime that will enable restoration of healthy ecological function.

description: This dataset displays tobacco-related deaths in the City of Austin between 2006 and 2018 and includes year of death, gender, age, race/ethnicity and whether tobacco contributed to the death (yes or probably contributed). Data are sourced from the City of Austin's Office of Vital Records. The contribution of tobacco to a death is indicated using a checkbox on the death certificate (marked by the individual filling out the death certificate). description: This table contains data from the community survey conducted as part of an Audit of the City's Cultural Centers. We surveyed members of the Austin community using a survey developed by the audit team. Survey questions generally asked respondents' opinions on cultural center programs, staff, fees, and facilities. The survey opened January 3 and closed January 27, 2020. name: Impervious Cover 2023
description: This dataset entails the delineation of impervious surfaces and artificial land cover types extracted from aerial imagery captured in 2023. columns.name: MAX_HEIGHT

columns.name: MODIFY_TYPE
columns.description: Describes whether the feature was identified as existing (1), updated (2), new(3), or delete (4) when comparing the previous dataset with the current aerial imagery. For example, if a feature from the 2017 impervious cover dataset didn't change based on the 2019 aerial imagery, the MODIFY_TYPE would be set to 1 (Existing).

columns.description: Area of the feature in square feet.

columns.description: The date the feature was last modified.

columns.name: MODIFIED_BY
columns.description: Identifies who last modified the feature.

columns.name: CREATED_DATE
columns.description: The date the feature was created.

name: Campaign Finance - Committee Purpose

columns.name: COR_Flag
columns.description:
If the transaction is reported on a Correction Affidavit, the correction flag will contain either NEW, or MODIFY.  

NEW indicates the transaction is newly reported, 

MODIFY indicates the transaction is a modification of a previously reported transaction.

columns.name: Purpose_ID
columns.description: An identifier associated with Committee Purposes - when combined with the Report_ID, serves as a unique identifier for each individual transaction.

columns.name: View_Report
columns.description: A link to view a pdf version of the report on which the transaction is listed

columns.name: Recipient
columns.description:
The name of the candidate or officeholder supported, opposed, or assisted by the committee during the reporting period, or if the committee assisted a political party the name of the party assisted. 

If the item opposed or supported was a ballot measure, the ballot ID (if applicable) will be reported in this column.

columns.name: Office_Held
columns.description: A description of the current office held by an officeholder supported, opposed or assisted by the committee.

columns.name: Filer_Name
columns.description: Name of individual or political committee filing the report. This name is a standardized format used by the Office of the City Clerk to ensure all of a filer's reports are accessible under a single name

columns.name: Measure_Description
columns.description: A brief description of the ballot measure supported, opposed or assisted by the committee.

columns.name: Committee_Activity
columns.description:
Identifies the activity of the committee

Support indicates the committee supported the candidate or ballot measure during the reporting period.

Oppose indicates the committee opposed the candidate or ballot measure during the reporting period.

Assist indicates the committee assisted an officeholder or political party during the reporting period.

columns.name: Election_Date
columns.description: The election date of the ballot measure supported or opposed by the committee.

columns.name: Committee_Purp_ID
columns.description: Unique ID based upon the concatenation of the Report_ID and Purpose_ID fields - acts as a unique key for each individual transaction

columns.name: Purpose_Type
columns.description: Identifies the purpose/recipient type of the committee activity:  a candidate or officeholder, or a ballot measure.

columns.name: Report
columns.description: Unique identifier for the specific report - can be used to link individual transactions to the report on which they appear.

columns.name: Office_Sought
columns.description: A description of the office sought by a candidate supported, opposed or assisted by the committee.

description: If a political committee has supported, opposed, or assisted a candidate, officeholder, or ballot measure during the reporting period, this activity will be reported in the Committee Purpose Dataset.

columns.name: Time_First_Unit_Enroute
columns.description: Time_First_Unit_Enroute (in minutes) states the length of time, in minutes, after the first ambulance was assigned and before the ambulance set off toward the emergency.

columns.name: Time_First_Unit_Assigned
columns.description: Time_First_Unit_Assigned (in minutes) states the length of time, in minutes, after the EMS call was picked up and before the first ambulance received notification of the emergency and was as- signed.

columns.name: Time_PhonePickUp_Date
columns.description: Time_PhonePickUp_Date states the date of the EMS call.

columns.name: Call_Disposition
columns.description: Call_Disposition states the final disposition of the event, such as cancelled call, transported to hospital, etc. If the ambulance transported the patient to a hospital, the name of the hospital would be specified. If the emergency call was from another government agency, for example, the Austin Fire Department, then the call disposition would be labelled as ”referred”. Other types of call dispositions fall into the category of ”defunct calls”; in this case, the call disposition of an incident would be labelled as its respective subcategory.

columns.name: Problem
columns.description: Problem states the problem type of the emergency.

columns.name: Priority_Number
columns.description: states the acuity of the emergency. A 1 indicates highest priority and a 15 would indicate least priority.

columns.name: Time_First_Unit_Arrived
columns.description: Time_First_Unit_Arrived (in minutes) states the length of time, in minutes, after the first ambulance wheels began to roll and before the ambulance arrived at the emergency and the wheels stopped.

description: Protected Streets are streets that have paved within the last five (residential) or seven (collector/arterial) years or have an RCI greater than seven (collector/arterial). There are strict guidelines for justification of cuts and restoration of Protected Streets. There is also a street damage recovery fee assessed for the life of the pavement that is lost. This product is for informational purposes and may not have been prepared for or be suitable for legal, engineering, or surveying purposes.

description: The ultimate goal of HIV treatment is to achieve viral suppression, which means the amount of HIV in the body is very low or undetectable. This is important for people with HIV to stay healthy, have improved quality of life, and live longer. People living with HIV who maintain viral suppression have effectively no risk of passing HIV to others. Texas DSHS is the source of this data. Diagnosed- received a diagnosis of HIV Linked to care*-visited an HIV heath care provider within 1 month (30 days)

name: Cultural Funding Awards
description: The City of Austin receives 7% of the Hotel Occupancy Tax. description: Dataset description - Wildfire mechanical fuel treatments are vegetation reduction projects also known as "Shaded fuel Breaks". Shaded fuel breaks are wildfire mitigation techniques used for wooded areas near homes and other structures. The foliage is reduced by trimming low-hanging branches, the clearing of undergrowth, and the removal of dead and downed trees. description: The EPA Energy Star Portfolio Manager is a nationally utilized online tool to measure and track energy and water consumption, as well as greenhouse gas emissions. The score, which ranges from 1 to 100 indicates the energy efficiency of a particular building. The score represents the percentile that a particular building falls in compared to similar buildings across the nation. A building with a score of 75 indicates that that building operates more energy efficiently than 75% of similar building

name: Number and percentage of people who successfully complete workforce development training, EOA.F.4

columns.name: Completion Rate - % completed
columns.description: This snapshot approach to enrollment and completion acknowledges that individuals participating in these programs may take time to complete them; the number of completers in relation to the number of enrollees in any given year does not represent a completion or persistence rate, since enrollees might be engaged in longer term training and completers in that year might have entered a program prior to that year. The reports calculate a completion rate which indicates whether enrollees in that year were found to have completed a program.

columns.name: Completion Rate - Total enrolled by Year
columns.description: Enrollment represents any individual enrolled in a program during that year, regardless of whether the individual was newly enrolled in that year or whether the individual had previously enrolled in a prior year but had not completed. Enrollment represents all individuals in the local workforce system during that year.

columns.name: Completion Rate - 1 year or less
columns.description: Completers include anyone who completed a program in that year, regardless of their entry period. So, an individual who started and completed a short-term training program is counted in completion numbers for that year and an individual who started a program three years prior to their completion year is counted in that year’s completion numbers.

columns.name: Completion Rate - 1 - 2 years
columns.description: Completers include anyone who completed a program in that year, regardless of their entry period. So, an individual who started and completed a short-term training program is counted in completion numbers for that year and an individual who started a program three years prior to their completion year is counted in that year’s completion numbers.

columns.name: Completion Rate - More than 2 years  (Completion Rate by year)
columns.description: Completers include anyone who completed a program in that year, regardless of their entry period. So, an individual who started and completed a short-term training program is counted in completion numbers for that year and an individual who started a program three years prior to their completion year is counted in that year’s completion numbers.

columns.name: Completers
columns.description: Completers include anyone who completed a program in that year, regardless of their entry period. So, an individual who started and completed a short-term training program is counted in completion numbers for that year and an individual who started a program three years prior to their completion year is counted in that year’s completion numbers.

description: This dataset is used to join Vision Zero crash records to mobility project records, so that they can be used in further analysis. The records are joined by selecting crashes whose locations fall within 40-feet of a project. This data is not meant to be useful for analysis on its own, but rather an intermediary step for linking and conducting analyses between crash records and mobility project records.

name: APD Use of Force
provider: City of Austin, Texas - Austin Police - data.austintexas.gov

columns.name: Firearm
columns.description: Indicates whether a firearm was used during the incident (1 = Yes, 0 = No).

columns.name: Weaponless
columns.description: Indicates whether physical force without weapons was used during the incident (1 = Yes, 0 = No).

columns.name: Impact Weapon
columns.description: Indicates whether an impact weapon was used during the incident (1 = Yes, 0 = No).

columns.name: Subject Case Number
columns.description: Unique identifier for a subject within an incident (combination of person ID and case number).

columns.name: Occurred Date
columns.description: The calendar date on which the incident occurred.

columns.name: Occurred Month
columns.description: The calendar month in which the incident occurred.

columns.name: Highest Subject Injury
columns.description: The most severe injury sustained by the subject during the incident.

columns.name: Subject Impairment
columns.description: The type of perceived impairment of the subject during the incident (emotionally disturbed/mentally unstable, under the influence of alcohol and/or drugs, both, or none observed).

columns.name: Canine
columns.description: Indicates whether a canine was deployed during the incident (1 = Yes, 0 = No).

columns.name: PIT
columns.description: Indicates whether a Precision Immobilization Technique (PIT) was used during the incident (1 = Yes, 0 = No).

columns.name: Pepperball
columns.description: Indicates whether a pepperball system was used during the incident (1 = Yes, 0 = No).

columns.name: Highest Subject Resistance
columns.description: The highest level of resistance demonstrated by the subject during the incident.

columns.name: Arrested
columns.description: Indicates whether the subject was arrested (1 = Yes, 0 = No).

columns.name: Taser
columns.description: Indicates whether a taser was used during the incident (1 = Yes, 0 = No).

columns.name: Highest Force Level
columns.description: The highest level of force used against the subject during the incident, categorized from Level 4 (least severe) to Level 1 (most severe).

columns.name: Chemical Agent
columns.description: Indicates whether a chemical agent was used during the incident (1 = Yes, 0 = No).

columns.name: Occurred Year
columns.description: The calendar year in which the incident occurred.

description: DATASET DESCRIPTION: The dataset is derived from reports submitted by officers for each subject against whom force was used. Because every officer involved in a use of force incident is required to complete a separate report for each subject, details about the situation and the subject’s behavior may vary across reports written by different officers. To produce subject-level data, certain fields were therefore aggregated, with values consolidated to reflect the highest reported level across all

name: Contract Desk Audits
description: The Austin Small and Minority Business Resources' Compliance Division conducts internal contract desk audits to verify compliance with Minority Business Enterprise (MBE) and Women Business Enterprise (WBE) Procurement Program, ensuring adherence with program requirements and participation agreements. This data represents the contract desk audits performed in the fiscal year. City of Austin Open Data Terms of Use https://data.austintexas.gov/stories/s/ranj-cccq

name: Outreach Events (Hosted & Attended)
description: The Austin Small and Minority Business Resources' Strategic Services Division manages the department’s communication, marketing, outreach; strategic planning and initiatives; process improvement and data reporting. The division is responsible for ensuring the department has representation at internal and external events during the fiscal year to promote the Minority-Owned and Women-Owned Business Enterprise Procurement Program, certification services and its supportive services program. columns.name: Female
columns.description: Count of female population for age range

columns.name: Male
columns.description: Count of male population for age range

columns.description: Age range

description: This dataset contains data by age and sex for the City of Austin, Travis County, Austin metro area, and Austin City Council Districts. The data source is the American Community Survey and is updated annually. The dataset is formatted for creation of population pyramid data visualizations.

name: Firms That Participated in a Supportive Service Program
description: The Austin Small and Minority Business Resources delivers a robust supportive services program. The Resources Division offers online workshops, training programs, technical assistance, panel discussions, networking events, and resources to the small business community. This data displays the participants who participated in supportive services programming during the fiscal year. City of Austin Open Data Terms of Use https://data.austintexas.gov/stories/s/ranj-cccq

name: Compliance Engagements in Project Meetings
description: The Austin Small and Minority Business Resources' Compliance Division participates in pre-bid, pre-construction, and progress meetings to support Minority Business Enterprise (MBE) and Women Business Enterprise (WBE) Procurement Program compliance and promote MBE/WBE participation throughout the project lifecycle. This data displays the project meetings ASMBR's Compliance Division performed engagement efforts. City of Austin Open Data Terms of Use https://data.austintexas.gov/stories/s/ranj-cccq

name: Green Building Mandatory
description: This shapefile shows the areas of Austin where buildings are required to achieve an Austin Energy Green Building Rating. These areas may be PUDs, zoning cases, development agreements, or other.

name: Firms Maintaining Certification
description: The Ausitn Small and Minority Business Resources' Certification Division maintains certification for minority-owned, women-owned, and disadvantage businesses. This data represents firms that held certification during the fiscal year, including firms whose term ended during the timeframe. City of Austin Open Data Terms of Use https://data.austintexas.gov/stories/s/ranj-cccq

name: Request for Council Actions (RCAs) Processed
description: The Small and Minority Business Resources Department’s Compliance Division evaluates procurement requests to ensure alignment with program participation goals prior to submission to City Council. City of Austin Open Data Terms of Use https://data.austintexas.gov/stories/s/ranj-cccq

name: New Applications Approved
description: The Small and Minority Business Resources Department’s Certification Division approves certifications for minority-owned, women-owned, and disadvantage businesses. The data represents certification applications approved by the Certification Division during the fiscal year. City of Austin Open Data Terms of Use https://data.austintexas.gov/stories/s/ranj-cccq

name: Program Compliance Violations Documented and Enforced
description: The Small and Minority Business Resources Department's Compliance Division monitors awarded contracts throughout all phases of the project to ensure compliance of the Minority-Owned and Women-Owned Business Enterprise Procurement Program. When a non-compliance violation occurs, prime contractors are notified of the violation and are provided the opportunity to file an appeal. Violations may be rescinded, upheld, or issued a warning letter. City of Austin Open Data Terms of Use https://data.austi

name: Compliant Bidders and Proposers Identified
description: The Austin Small and Minority Business Resources' Compliance Division performs compliance review and monitoring for pre-solicitation issuance through contract closeout. This data represents bidders and proposers (prime contractors) compliant with program requirements, and the percentage of MBE/WBE goals. City of Austin Open Data Terms of Use https://data.austintexas.gov/stories/s/ranj-cccq

name: ACAO Efficiency Initiative Inventory

columns.name: Name of Initiative
columns.description: The name of the efficiency initiative.

columns.name: Submitting Department
columns.description: The primary department that is responsible for submitting and implementing the efficiency initiative

columns.name: Initiative Source
columns.description: The source of the initiative. Options are: Department-led, Legal or Regulatory Change, City Council, Audit Recommendation, Consultant Report, Investigation, Other.

columns.name: Estimated Financial Impacts
columns.description: The estimated financial savings or increased revenue from this initiative.

columns.name: ID
columns.description: Used internally to track all initiatives submitted to ACAO

columns.name: Link to Report
columns.description: Link to associated report, memo, or supporting document.

columns.name: Additional Impacts
columns.description: Other operational efficiencies from this initiative other than direct financial impacts.

columns.name: Status
columns.description: The current status of the initiative as a whole. Options are: Planned, Underway, Completed, No work planned.

columns.name: Initiative Type
columns.description: The initiative type that best describe the efficiency initiative. Options are: Process Improvement, Technology-driven Improvement, Procurement and Vendor Management, Workforce & Org Changes, Policy and Compliance Adjustment, Revenue Enhancement and Cost Recovery, Sustainability and Resource Management, Other.

columns.name: Summary of Initiative
columns.description: A brief description of the efficiency initiative.

columns.name: Date of Initiative
columns.description: The date the initiative was started, or the report was accepted by the department (not necessarily fully implemented).

columns.name: Realized Financial Impacts
columns.description: The realized cost savings or increased revenue from this initiative.

columns.name: Verified
columns.description: Displays if the reported initiative has been verified by staff in the Austin City Auditor's Office.

description: This dataset is an inventory of efficiency initiatives submitted by departments across the City of Austin. The dataset collects financial and operational impacts of these initiatives, such as estimated and realized cost savings, time savings, service-quality improvements, and other measurable outcomes.

name: Austin Public Library Visitors
description: Number of visitor to each library location by month.

name: Monthly Circulation by Library
description: Information about the circulation of physical materials at the Austin Public Library. Metrics include: material type and audience. Audience is broken down by Adult, Children or Teen. There's a total for Adults, and a Youth total that combines Children and Teen.

name: Austin Public Library WiFi Usage
description: Number of public WiFi connections and data usage at Austin Public Library locations. Total WiFi usage is in gigabytes.

name: Austin Public Library Computer Usage
description: Use of public computers at the Austin Public Library. Metrics include: Sessions - number of logins, Total Session Time (in Hours) - how long the computers were used. Each metric is broken down by computer area and location.
